2604.07223.title: TraceSafe: A Systematic Assessment of LLM Guardrails on Multi-Step Tool-Calling Trajectories
2604.07223.paper_summary: As large language models (LLMs) evolve from static chatbots into autonomous agents, the primary vulnerability surface shifts from final outputs to intermediate execution traces. While safety guardrails are well-benchmarked for natural language responses, their efficacy remains largely unexplored within multi-step tool-use trajectories. To address this gap, we introduce TraceSafe-Bench, the first comprehensive benchmark specifically designed to assess mid-trajectory safety. It encompasses 12 risk categories, ranging from security threats (e.g., prompt injection, privacy leaks) to operational failures (e.g., hallucinations, interface inconsistencies), featuring over 1,000 unique execution instances. Our evaluation of 13 LLM-as-a-guard models and 7 specialized guardrails yields three critical findings: 1) Structural Bottleneck: Guardrail efficacy is driven more by structural data competence (e.g., JSON parsing) than semantic safety alignment. Performance correlates strongly with structured-to-text benchmarks ($ρ=0.79$) but shows near-zero correlation with standard jailbreak robustness. 2) Architecture over Scale: Model architecture influences risk detection performance more significantly than model size, with general-purpose LLMs consistently outperforming specialized safety guardrails in trajectory analysis. 3) Temporal Stability: Accuracy remains resilient across extended trajectories. Increased execution steps allow models to pivot from static tool definitions to dynamic execution behaviors, actually improving risk detection performance in later stages. Our findings suggest that securing agentic workflows requires jointly optimizing for structural reasoning and safety alignment to effectively mitigate mid-trajectory risks.
2604.07223.paper_summary_zh: 隨著大型語言模型（LLMs）從靜態聊天機器人演變為自主代理，其主要脆弱性面向從最終輸出轉移到中間執行痕跡。雖然安全防護措施在自然語言回應中已經有良好的基準，但其在多步驟工具使用過程中的有效性仍然 largely 未被探索。為了解決這一空白，我們推出了 TraceSafe-Bench，這是第一個專門設計用來評估中途安全的綜合基準。它涵蓋了 12 個風險類別，從安全威脅（例如，提示注入、隱私洩漏）到操作失敗（例如，幻覺、介面不一致），並包含超過 1,000 個獨特的執行實例。我們對 13 個 LLM 作為防護模型和 7 個專門的防護措施的評估得出了三個關鍵發現：1）結構瓶頸：防護措施的有效性更多地受到結構數據能力（例如，JSON 解析）的驅動，而非語義安全對齊。性能與結構到文本基準的相關性強（$ρ=0.79$），但與標準越獄穩健性幾乎沒有相關性。2）架構優於規模：模型架構對風險檢測性能的影響比模型大小更為顯著，通用 LLM 在軌跡分析中始終優於專門的安全防護措施。3）時間穩定性：準確性在延長的軌跡中保持穩定。增加的執行步驟使模型能夠從靜態工具定義轉向動態執行行為，實際上在後期階段提高了風險檢測性能。我們的發現表明，確保代理工作流程需要共同優化結構推理和安全對齊，以有效減輕中途風險。
2604.07223.author: Yen-Shan Chen et.al.
2604.07223.authors: Yen-Shan Chen, Sian-Yao Huang, Cheng-Lin Yang, Yun-Nung Chen
